name: Personal-Trainer / Coach Discovery Call: Certifications, Methodology, Fit
description: Place a discovery call with a personal trainer, strength coach, or remote fitness coach to verify certifications, probe methodology, surface package structure / commitment lengths / cancellation, and assess fit BEFORE the operator pays for a package or signs a multi-month commitment.
disclaimer: Personal training is an unregulated field in most jurisdictions. 'Certified' alone is meaningless — the issuing body matters. This skill does not give medical clearance; if the operator has cardiac, orthopaedic, neurological, or pregnancy-related concerns, they should clear with a physician first.

context.when_to_use: Operator is choosing between trainers (in-person, hybrid, or online-only) and wants to vet a candidate before paying for a package. Useful for new-to-training operators, return-from-injury, sport-specific goals, weight-loss / body-composition goals, and post-rehab strength work.
preconditions: Operator has clear goals stated in 2-3 sentences (e.g. 'add 10 lb of muscle in 6 months', 'run sub-25 5k', 'return to lifting after L4-L5 disc protrusion cleared by PT').
Operator has training history summary (years training, current routine, recent injuries).
Operator has a realistic time-per-week and budget-per-month range.
Operator has medical clearance for vigorous exercise, OR a flagged contraindication for the trainer to acknowledge.

principles: Certification body matters: NSCA-CSCS, NASM-CPT, ACSM-CPT, ACE, PN, RKC, USA-W are reputable; weekend-warrior certificates are not. Coaches without exam-based certs need a strong track record to compensate.
Methodology fit is more predictive than certifications. A great hypertrophy coach is wrong for an ultrarunner. Ask what kind of clients the coach gets results with.
Programming, not personality, is what the operator is paying for. Ask to see a sample programme or block structure for someone with similar goals.
Communication channels and response times define the experience. Slack, app, text, weekly check-ins — pin them down.
Multi-month packages without a kill-switch are a major risk in a deregulated market. Cancellation, refunds, and credit-for-unused sessions belong in writing.
Scope of practice. A trainer is not a physiotherapist, dietitian, or psychologist. Coaches who 'diagnose your bloodwork' or write meal plans without a dietitian credential are out of scope.

phrases.opener: Hi — calling for someone who's looking for a coach for [goal in one sentence]. Got a few minutes? I'd like to ask a bit about your background and how you usually work with people.
phrases.credentials_probe: Could you tell me which certifications you hold and from which bodies, plus any specialty creds — corrective exercise, pre/post-natal, athletic performance, nutrition? And how long you've been coaching full-time?
phrases.methodology_probe: What's your programming philosophy in 2-3 sentences? Who's the operator-profile you get the strongest results with, and what does that programme typically look like?
phrases.similar_client_probe: Could you describe a recent client similar to the operator — their starting point, the programme you ran, and the outcome at 12 weeks? Anonymous is fine.
phrases.scope_probe: What's in scope and out of scope for your coaching — programming, technique coaching via video, nutrition guidance, recovery, sleep? And where do you refer out?
phrases.package_probe: How do packages work — month-to-month, 3-month minimum, 6-month commitment? What's billed, when, and through what platform?
phrases.cancellation_probe: What's the cancellation policy mid-package? Can the operator pause for travel or injury? Are unused sessions refundable or credited?
phrases.communication_probe: What channels do we use between sessions, and what's your realistic response time? How often are programme reviews and adjustments?
phrases.medical_clearance_probe: The operator has [specific consideration: 'recent disc injury cleared by PT', 'mild hypertension', 'past ankle reconstruction']. How do you typically work with someone in that situation, and do you require physician clearance?
phrases.sample_request: Could you send a sample first-week programme for an operator with these goals, plus a one-page bio with your certifications and a couple of references?
phrases.stall_to_check: Let me share the package details with the operator before we commit — they'll want to see the cancellation terms in writing.
phrases.graceful_close: Thank you — please email a sample programme, the package terms in writing, and the cancellation policy. The operator will review and follow up within [timeframe].

tactics.name: Verify certifications and tenure
tactics.script: Use `credentials_probe`. Note any reputable body (NSCA, NASM, ACSM, ACE, PN, RKC). Be politely sceptical of weekend-only certs as the sole credential.

tactics.name: Probe methodology with a real comparable
tactics.script: Use `methodology_probe` and `similar_client_probe`. The pattern is: who do you serve best, what programme do you actually run, what did the last similar client's 12 weeks look like? Vague answers are a signal.

tactics.name: Stay in scope
tactics.script: Use `scope_probe`. Coaches who write meal plans (without RD credential), interpret bloodwork, or treat injuries are out of bounds. A good coach knows where to refer.

tactics.name: Surface medical considerations early
tactics.script: Use `medical_clearance_probe` for any flagged concern. A coach who responds 'sure I can train through that' without clearance or modification protocol is a red flag.

tactics.name: Package and cancellation in writing
tactics.script: Use `package_probe` and `cancellation_probe`. Get the cancellation policy in writing before any payment. Refundable / credit-for-unused / pause-for-travel are the three knobs to confirm.

tactics.name: Request a sample programme
tactics.script: Use `sample_request`. A coach who has nothing to show is either new or coasting. A one-week sample isn't proprietary — it's a sales tool.

tactics.name: Defer commitment
tactics.when: Coach pitches a discount for signing today.
tactics.script: Use `stall_to_check`. 'Sign now and save' is a sales tactic. Real coaches honour their pricing for at least 48 hours.

boundaries: Do NOT sign a multi-month commitment or pay for a package mid-call. Use ask_operator.
Do NOT provide credit card or bank details to 'hold the spot'. Reputable coaches do not require deposits to schedule a first paid session.
Do NOT share the operator's full medical history with a coach who lacks the credentials to act on it. Share what's relevant to programming.
Do NOT accept nutrition prescription or bloodwork interpretation from a coach without dietitian / clinical credentials.
Do NOT bypass medical clearance the operator's physician has indicated is needed.
Do NOT engage a coach whose methodology centres on supplements they sell, MLM products, or proprietary food / device purchases.
success_signals: Verifiable certifications from reputable bodies, named on the call.
Clear ideal-client profile that maps to the operator's goal.
Specific recent client comparable, with starting point, programme, and outcome.
Scope clearly bounded — refers out for nutrition / rehab / mental health.
Written package terms with cancellation, refund / credit, and pause clauses.
Sample programme will be sent before commitment.
Acknowledges medical considerations with a sensible plan (modify, clear with physician, refer).
failure_signals: Cannot describe a typical programme structure or block.
Multi-month commitment with no cancellation or pause language.
Out-of-scope offers (nutrition prescription, bloodwork reading, supplement sales).
Discount-pressure to sign on the call.
Dismissive of operator's flagged injury, condition, or physician guidance.
Refusal to share a sample programme or references.

exit_strategy.on_success: Confirm coach will email: sample first-week programme, bio with certifications, written package terms, cancellation policy, references, and the platform/app stack. Operator reviews and decides within [timeframe]. Note rate, billing cadence, communication channels, and named scope.
exit_strategy.on_failure: Politely close. If the failure was scope-related, ask if the coach can refer to a colleague who handles the operator's goal. Report back with specifics on why this candidate isn't a fit.
follow_ups: Email the operator: coach name, certifications, methodology fit assessment, package terms, cancellation policy, sample programme attached, red/green flags, recommended next step.
If proceeding: schedule the first paid session AFTER package terms are reviewed; never before.
Calendar a 4-week and 12-week check-in to assess fit independently of the coach's self-assessment.

required_user_info: Goal in 2-3 sentences: outcome + deadline + constraint
Training history: years training, current routine, performance markers
Recent or ongoing injuries / medical considerations and any physician clearance status
Time available per week and preferred days/times
Budget per month and willingness for a multi-month commitment
In-person, hybrid, or fully remote preference
Operator's preferred communication channels and response-time expectations
